title: Fix MongoDB Query Projection to Handle Missing Fields

statement.markdown: Write a function to implement a MongoDB query projection for a given document. The function should project only the fields that exist in the document and ignore missing fields. If a field has a nested sub-document, the function should project only the fields within the nested sub-document if they exist.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object representing a MongoDB document
statement.outputFormat: A JSON object representing the projected document
statement.notes: The function should handle documents with arbitrary nesting and missing fields.

constraints.description: Number of fields in the document

examples.explanation: The function projects only the fields that exist in the document, ignoring the missing field "state" within the nested "address" sub-document.
The function projects all fields in the document, including the nested fields within the "address" sub-document.

hints.text: You can use a recursive function to handle nested sub-documents.

editorial.title: Editorial: Handling Missing Fields in MongoDB Query Projection
editorial.intuition: To project missing fields, you can use a recursive function to traverse the document and check for existing fields.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve iterating over each field in the document and checking for its existence.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a recursive function to handle nested sub-documents.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive function to traverse the document and project only existing fields.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the recursive function correctly handles nested sub-documents and missing fields.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run would involve testing the function with various inputs to ensure it correctly projects missing fields.

starterCode.javascript: // Define a function to project missing fields from a MongoDB document
